content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks that rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement framework that maintains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data. This adapted framework would avoid the common stagnation pitfall of single-perspective self-refinement where an LLM cannot escape its own initial biased reasoning flaws.
